issues.detail: gloss_en is 'local gravity' but Ortskraft means 'local employee/staff'. gloss_en_full 'Local employee' is correct but gloss_en is wrong.
Current synonyms_en [rip, snag, hole, damage] are not synonyms of 'crease (clothing)'. A Bruch in fabric is a fold/crease, not a rip or hole. Replaced with accurate synonyms.

All annotations are in English — annotated the translation text instead of the German sentence. Forms are 'Interest rates', 'will', 'stay', 'low' instead of German words 'Zinsen', 'bleiben', 'niedrig' etc.

Multiple annotation errors: (1) 'Eimer' has English lemma 'fool' instead of 'Eimer', (2) 'ich' is pos 'noun' with gloss_hint 'we' — should not be annotated (pronoun), (3) 'mein'' has lemma 'mein'' instead of 'meinen', (4) 'Salz' has English lemma 'salt' instead of 'Salz'.
Annotation for 'Bund' has gloss_hint 'federation' but this example is about a pipe collar (sense: Verstärkung am Ende eines Rohres). gloss_hint should be 'collar'.
Translation 'I am currently on the way according to plan' is wrong. 'nach Plan' here is a place name (the proper noun Plan). Should be 'I am currently on the way to Plan.'
Annotation for 'Plan' has pos 'noun' and gloss_hint 'plan' but this is a proper noun (place name). Should have pos 'noun' with gloss_hint 'Plan (place name)' or reference names/Plan.

Translation 'the plan of the previous century' is wrong. 'das Plan' with neuter article refers to the place name Plan, not a plan/scheme. Should be 'the Plan of the previous century' (referring to the locality).

Translation says '(failing grade)' but context 'Der Fünfer fährt in die Innenstadt' refers to bus/tram line, not a grade. gloss_hint 'five (failing grade)' is wrong — should match sense 'five (number)' or have no hint.
Translation incorrectly says 'from the fork (utensil)' — Gabel here means Astgabel (tree fork/branch fork), sense 0. The person is standing in a tree fork, not on a utensil.
Annotation for 'Auge' has English lemma 'eye' instead of German lemma 'Auge'.
Annotation has form 'dorp' but the German text says 'Dorf'. 'dorp' is not in the sentence.
Annotation for 'zugefallen' has lemma 'zugefallen' (past participle) — correct lemma should be 'zufallen' (infinitive).
Annotation for 'eben' has gloss_hint 'level' but here 'eben' is an adverb meaning 'just/precisely', not the adjective 'level/flat'.
Annotation for 'fünf' has English lemma 'five' instead of German lemma 'fünf'.
Annotation for 'Ende' has English lemma 'end' instead of German lemma 'Ende'. Also annotation for 'geboren' is wrong — the text says 'gehoben' (aus der Taufe gehoben), not 'geboren'.
All annotations are in English — annotated the translation text instead of the German sentence. Forms are 'Her', 'long', 'black' instead of German words 'Ihre', 'langen', 'schwarzen' etc.
Annotation for 'Formation' has English lowercase lemma 'formation' instead of German 'Formation'. Also gloss_hint is a pipe-delimited list of all senses instead of just 'military unit'.
Annotation for 'der' has English lemma 'the' instead of German 'der'. Also 'Formationen' has gloss_hint 'category' which doesn't match any sense — context is geological/vegetation formations.
Annotation for 'Sie' has English lemma 'she' instead of German 'sie'.
Annotation for 'Hüte' has lemma 'Hut' (noun: hat) but here 'Hüte dich' is the imperative of 'hüten' (to beware). Lemma should be 'hüten', pos 'verb'.
Annotation for 'Sie' has pos 'noun' with gloss_hint 'she' — should not be annotated (pronoun). Also 'da' has pos 'adjective' but is an adverb here.
Annotation for 'Ich' has pos 'noun' with gloss_hint 'I (pronoun)' — should not be annotated (pronoun).
Annotation for 'Das' has pos 'pronoun' with gloss_hint 'which (nominative)' — 'Das' here is a determiner article, not a relative pronoun.
